plang_failures_note: All 6 PLang failures are intentional fail-fixtures under Tests/TestModule/Report/_fixtures_fail/ and _fixtures_sensitive/ (failsvar asserts 42==99; sensitivefail asserts identity=='will-not-match'). They exist to be failed-by-design so the Report module's tests can verify failure reporting. `plang --test` discovers them directly because of the leading-underscore fixture convention. NOT coder regressions — pre-existing, not touched behaviorally on this branch.

coverage.note: Line coverage on the changed FS files is healthy (Path.Authorize.cs 98.4%, Path.Operations.cs 83.2%). The problem is NOT line coverage — it is behavioral verification. See findings 1 and 2: lines run green while the property they were changed to enforce is never exercised.

findings.issue: v3's ONLY behavioral change is PLangFileSystem.cs:227 — `path.StartsWith(RootDirectory, OrdinalIgnoreCase)` became `... Path.RootComparison` (OS-aware: Ordinal on Linux). No test exercises a path whose prefix is a CASE-VARIANT of the root. Every in-root test uses exact casing (matches under both comparisons); every out-of-root test uses a wholly distinct path (`//tmp/...`, matches under neither). Reverting line 227 to `OrdinalIgnoreCase` fails ZERO tests. The line is 100% line-covered and branch-covered — pure coverage dazzle. Same blind spot applies to Path.Authorize.IsInRoot (RootComparison at Path.Authorize.cs:95-96).
findings.impact: This is the permission gate. The case-comparison rule exists so `/SRV/myapp` cannot slip past the gate by matching root `/srv/myapp` on a case-sensitive Linux filesystem. A future edit that reverts this to case-insensitive comparison = sandbox escape (out-of-root path auto-granted as in-root), and the green suite would not notice. The exact regression codeanalyzer v2 #2 was raised to fix has no regression test.
findings.suggestion: Add to ValidatePathTests (Linux-gated, like the existing OsRooted tests): create app with root `<tmp>/plang-vp-abc`, call ValidatePath on `<tmp>/plang-vp-ABC/file.txt` (uppercased segment), assert the result is returned UNCHANGED (out-of-root => normaliser-only path, line 229). Under the OrdinalIgnoreCase bug it would fall into the in-root resolution branch and be rewritten. Add the mirror to PathAuthorizeTests: a Path under a case-variant of fs.RootDirectory must NOT be auto-granted (must prompt / Find).

findings.issue: No test verifies a Move actually moves. `Move_OneMissingGrant` and `Move_BothPathsMissing` assert only result.Success, channel AskCount, and question text — never that the source file is gone and the destination exists. `Copy_MirrorsMove` DOES assert both files exist (correct for copy). PerformTransfer (Path.Operations.cs:228-235) branches on `isMove`: line 233 `File.Move` vs line 234 `File.Copy`. Flip the `isMove` flag and the entire suite stays green — the Move tests never look at the filesystem, and the Copy test's `src exists` assertion is true for a broken move-as-copy too.
findings.impact: MoveTo could silently behave as CopyTo (leaving the source behind) and no test would catch it. The defining difference between Move and Copy is unverified.
findings.suggestion: In Move_BothPathsMissing (and/or Move_OneMissingGrant) after the await, assert `System.IO.File.Exists(srcFile)` IsFalse and `System.IO.File.Exists(dstFile)` IsTrue, and assert dst content equals the written source content. That pins the isMove=true branch.

findings.issue: 6 of the 8 PLang permission `.test.goal` files are named after Stage 5 / 2b permission scenarios but their bodies do a trivial IN-ROOT file round-trip (`save fixture.txt` -> `file.read fixture.txt` -> `assert equals`). In-root paths auto-grant (Path.Authorize:35) — these goals never reach a prompt, a suspend, a revoke, or a narrowed grant. `plang --test` prints `[Pass] Permission/RestartStillNoPrompt/...` etc., so the runner reports green tests under permission-scenario names that exercise none of those scenarios. The coder's in-goal comments honestly say 'placeholder' / 'exercised by C# scenario X' — but the goal NAME is what a CI dashboard, future auditor, or `--test` consumer sees. Only GrantAStoresPersisted and Authorize/StatefulAuthorizeGrantsAndContinues are real (out-of-root //tmp paths + answerer channels — verified .pr is correct).
findings.impact: Anyone auditing 'is the permission flow covered at the PLang layer?' sees 8 green goals with scenario names and concludes yes. Reality: 6 would pass even if the entire Path.Authorize gate were deleted. A misleadingly-named placeholder also squats the namespace slot so nobody writes the real test.
findings.suggestion: Either (a) rename the 6 placeholder goals to describe what they actually do (e.g. InRootReadRoundTrip) so the green is honest, or (b) delete them — the C# Stage5MessagesEndToEndTests cover scenarios 1/2/3/5/6 properly. Do not keep a goal whose name claims coverage that only exists one layer down in C#.

findings.issue: Scenario4_RestartStillNoPrompt_PersistedGrantSurvivesNewApp has an empty body (`await Task.CompletedTask;`) and asserts nothing. It reports as PASSED, not skipped. The coder documented the underlying deserialiser bug honestly in the summary, but the test row is a green with a scenario name.
findings.impact: The green count includes a test that verifies nothing; a reader scanning the suite believes restart-persistence is covered.
findings.suggestion: Mark it `[Skip("cross-App SettingsStore round-trip — deserialiser stack overflow in SmallObjectWithParameterizedConstructorConverter; see coder summary")]` so it reports as skipped, not passed.

findings.issue: IdempotentAdd_SamePathTwice_Overwrites_NoDuplicateRow asserts only that Find returns non-null after two Adds. If Add appended a duplicate instead of overwriting, Find would still return non-null and the test passes — the 'NoDuplicateRow' / 'Overwrites' claim in the name is unverified. Likewise TwoHomes_InMemoryGrant_AndPersistedGrant_FindReturnsCorrectOne asserts both grants are non-null but never verifies routing (in-memory grant from the session home, persisted grant from sqlite) — both could be served from one home and it passes.
findings.impact: A regression that double-stores grants, or that routes signed/unsigned grants to the wrong home, would not be caught.
findings.suggestion: IdempotentAdd: after both Adds, assert the store holds exactly one row for the path (count via the storage surface, or Revoke once then assert Find is null — proving there was no second copy). TwoHomes: assert `mem.RawSignature` is null (unsigned => in-memory) and `disk.RawSignature` is not null (signed => sqlite).

findings.issue: LegacyFsGoalTests_StayGreen_AgainstV2Surface — the name promises it pins legacy `.test.goal` fixtures against the v2 surface, but the body only asserts `RootDirectory != null` and `ValidatePath("/test") != null`. ValidatePath now always returns non-null (it is a pure normaliser post-branch), so the assertion is a near-tautology that exercises no legacy goal.
findings.impact: Negligible bug-catching power; the test name oversells. Harmless but it pads the green count.
findings.suggestion: Either delete it (the real legacy-vs-v2 coexistence is covered by the rest of the suite running green) or rename to `ValidatePath_StillResolves_AfterV2SurfaceAdded` so the name matches the trivial assertion.

findings.issue: IsInRoot() has two clauses: `IsUnder(fs.RootDirectory)` and `IsUnder(fs.OsDirectory)` (Path.Authorize.cs:95-96). The OsDirectory clause — auto-granting system-built-in goal files (test/build) that live outside the actor root — is never exercised by a test. FileSystemPermissionFlowTests.InRootPath only covers the RootDirectory clause.
findings.impact: If the OsDirectory short-circuit broke, system goal files would start prompting for permission and no test would catch it.
findings.suggestion: Add a PathAuthorizeTests case: a Path under `fs.OsDirectory` (but outside RootDirectory) returns Ok() from Authorize with no channel Ask.

findings.issue: Move/Copy bundled consent is only tested with answer 'a'. The 'n' branch (Path.Operations.cs:201-205 — PermissionDenied built from whichever path lacks the grant) and the stateless-channel branch (bundled Data<Ask> + Snapshot) are untested. Single-path flow tests both 'n' and stateless; bundled-consent does not.
findings.impact: A refused or suspended Move/Copy could mis-report which path was denied, or fail to bundle the Ask, without a test failing.
findings.suggestion: Add: Move with answer 'n' => result.Success false, Error IsTypeOf PermissionDenied, and assert Error.Permission.Path is the missing-grant path. Add: Move over a StatelessChannel => result.Type.Value == 'ask' && Snapshot not null.

findings.issue: Process violation: no `baseline-tests.md` exists in any coder version directory. The tester workflow depends on it to separate coder-introduced regressions from pre-existing failures. I reconstructed the distinction manually (clean rebuild + fixture-provenance check) and confirmed no regressions, but the safety net was absent.
findings.impact: On a branch with real green->red movement this gap would make regression attribution guesswork.
findings.suggestion: Coder must write `v<N>/baseline-tests.md` recording pre-work suite state before starting each version.

verdict_note: All 9 findings are TEST-quality gaps — none is a code bug. codeanalyzer v3 already passed the code, and the v3 fix is correct. But the suite is dishonest in three material ways: (1) v3's entire behavioral change has zero regression protection on a security gate, (2) Move's defining behavior is unverified, (3) six PLang goals report green under permission-scenario names they do not exercise. Findings 1-3 are cheap to fix (a handful of test additions/renames) and high-value.
summary: Clean rebuild: C# 2846/2846 pass; PLang 213 pass, 6 fail (all intentional fail-fixtures, not regressions). No regressions vs the implied baseline. Coverage on changed FS files is healthy on paper (Path.Authorize.cs 98.4%) — and that is precisely the trap: the v2 #2 fix (ValidatePath case-comparison) is 100% line- and branch-covered yet behaviorally unverified — reverting it fails nothing. Move semantics are unverified (flip isMove, suite stays green). Six of eight PLang permission .test.goal files do in-root round-trips under names that promise out-of-root/suspend/revoke/narrowing scenarios. Verdict: needs-fixes — three major false-greens plus minor weak assertions and one process violation (missing baseline-tests.md).
